About the Board of Supervisors

Learn about the Board of Supervisors and County Districts.

Who we are

The five members of the Board of Supervisors serve as the legislative and executive body of Marin County. Supervisors are elected by district to serve a four-year term. 

  • District 1: Supervisor Mary Sackett
  • District 2: Supervisor Brian Colbert (2nd Vice President)
  • District 3: Supervisor Stephanie Moulton-Peters (1st Vice President)
  • District 4: Supervisor Dennis Rodoni
  • District 5: Supervisor Eric Lucan (President)

What we do

The Board of Supervisors has the overall responsibility for the function of county government. 

  • The Board serves as the governing board of many special districts such as Marin County Open Space District, Marin Transit, and the Marin Housing Authority.
  • The Board passes all ordinances governing the county and is responsible for seeing that mandated functions are properly discharged.
  • The Board adopts the County budget; sets employee salaries and directs a wide variety of services to county residents.

Where we serve

Marin County is part of several regional and multi-county special districts. In addition to serving on the Marin County Board of Supervisors, members of the Board also serve on external City, Regional, State, and National boards and commissions, including (partial list):

  • Association of Bay Area Governments (ABAG)
  • Bay Area Air Quality Management District
  • Bay Conservation and Development Commission (BCDC)
  • California Coastal Commission (CCC)
  • California State Association of Counties (CSAC)
  • Golden Gate Bridge, Highway & Transportation District
  • Metropolitan Transportation Commission
  • Marin LAFCo
  • MCE Clean Energy
  • North Bay Water Reuse Authority
  • Richardson Bay Regional Agency (RBRA)
  • Sonoma-Marin Area Rail Transit (SMART)
  • Workforce Alliance of the North Bay

Related

  • District 1 — Mary Sackett

    Learn about Marin County Supervisor Mary Sackett. District 1 includes areas in and around San Rafael.
  • District 2 — Brian Colbert

    Learn about Marin County Supervisor Brian Colbert. District 2 includes the towns of Fairfax, Kentfield, Greenbrae, San Anselmo, and Ross. It includes part of the City of Larkspur and part of the City of San Rafael.
  • District 3 — Stephanie Moulton-Peters

    Learn about Marin County Supervisor Stephanie Moulton-Peters. District 3 includes Sausalito, Floating Homes, Marin City, Mill Valley, Tam Valley, Almonte, Alto-Sutton Manor, Strawberry, Tiburon, and Belvedere.
  • District 4 — Dennis Rodoni

    Learn about Marin County Supervisor Dennis Rodoni. District 4 includes West Marin as well as parts of San Rafael and east Marin.
  • District 5 — Eric Lucan

    Learn about Marin County Supervisor Eric Lucan. District 5 includes the majority of the City of Novato and the unincorporated communities of Bel Marin Keys, Loma Verde, Black Point, Green Point, Rush Creek/Atherton Corridor, and Indian Valley.
  • Beyond the Board: Conversations with Your Supervisor

    Beyond the Board: Conversations with Your Supervisor. A podcast spotlighting the programs, people, and priorities shaping Marin County. 

Contact

  • The Board of Supervisors
  • 415-473-7331
    To use the California Relay Service (CRS), dial 711
  • Email The Board of Supervisors
  • 3501 Civic Center Drive
    Suite 329
    San Rafael CA 94903
  • Mon - Fri: 8:30 am-5:00 pm
  • Board of Supervisors website

Connect to County of Marin

If you are a person with a disability and require an accommodation to participate in a County program, service, or activity, requests may be made by calling 415-473-4381 (Voice), Dial 711 for CA Relay, or by submitting a Disability Accommodation Request at least five business days in advance of the event. We will do our best to fulfill requests received with less than five business days’ notice. Copies of documents are available in alternative formats upon request.
